calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2026 Continued To Jul-14-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Enter Judgment Under Stipulation
Off Calendar Mar-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff JP Morgan Chase Bank, N.A.'s unopposed Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Enter Judgment Under Stipulation is GRANTED.

Plaintiff's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted.

Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered as such: Plaintiff Capital One, N.A. shall recover $7,176.82 in principal, and $0.00 in costs, for a total of $7,176.82.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form.

No proposed order and no proposed judgment received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order and proposed judgment which repeat verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Continued To Apr-14-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-13-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-10-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Feb-10-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Plaintiff JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s unopposed Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate a right to judgment in its favor. Plaintiff's motion turns on requests for admission to Defendant that have been deemed admitted. Plaintiff sought judicial notice of the order deeming the requests admitted, but did not seek notice (or even submit) of the requests, themselves. The court is left without sufficient information on this motion to find Defendant admitted all facts material to the specific judgment sought.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Dec-03-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

Ordered off calendar for noncompliance with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). Upon compliance, motion may be re-set for any court day after December 9. Papers shall bear new hearing date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2024 Set For Trial On Mar-24-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Dffmed Admitted
Off Calendar Sep-25-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter.

RULING: The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'s Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted is GRANTED. Requests for Admission were served on Defendant RYAN S. ODONNELL on June 6, 2024 to which no responses were received, said matters set forth in the Requests for Admissions are ORDERED deemed admitted.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Van Zandt; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 525.
